I'm So Me
(Im $o Me)

Notes:
(July 18, 2019) (Different Creature is officially released)
(December 26, 2019) (I'm So Me is officially released)
Type: I’m So Me is the second mixtape by Portland rapper Yeat, originally released on December 27, 2019. The tape would be Yeat’s last project of a prolific 2019. The project follows his earlier 2019 mixtape Wake Up Call and his EPs Deep Blue $trips and Different Creature with Clibbo. On January 3, 2020, I'm So Me was reuploaded with "Complicated" removed from the tracklist. On September 21, 2022, I’m So Me was removed from major streaming services and became exclusive to SoundCloud.

We Us

Notes:
(December 26, 2019) (I'm So Me is officially released)
(April 18, 2020) (We Us is officially released)
Type: We Us is the third EP by Portland rapper Yeat. It ties the record for his shortest EP, with three tracks, alongside his debut release Deep Blue $trips. His first project of 2020, the EP would be dropped in April and followed by another EP in September, Hold On. Due to the nature of the title, the project can be seen as falling in between I’m So Me and Up 2 Më in Yeat’s Me/We series.

Type: Hold Ön is Yeat’s 4th EP after We Us and Different Creature. This EP used to be on all platforms but is now only available on SoundCloud due to unknown reasons. This EP contains 4 songs, but only 2 songs are in the album version on SoundCloud, you can find the other 2 songs if you scroll down far enough in Yeat’s tracks. The 2 songs that are available on the album version on SoundCloud is “Nobu” and “Geeker Day”. This and We Us were only released due to issues Yeat was having at the time with his label. So he decided to drop these 2 EP’s in order to keep his fans from waiting a long time for another project.

Name:

(4L with us)
℗ 2021 TwizzyRich
Notes:
(April 2, 2021) (Alivë is officially released)
(June 10, 2021) (4L is officially released)
Type: 4L, originally titled 4L with us, is Yeat’s fourth mixtape, released on June 10, 2021, and the follow-up to his April 2021 project, Alivë. The project gained traction when the track “Sorry Bout That” took off, accompanied by a music video and TikTok popularity. The mixtape was submitted on June 4, 2021. This era was very prolific for Yeat, as in an Instagram Live on June 7, 2021 he revealed he had made 175 songs just in the past 30 days.

Name:

(Up 2 Më, My Lyfe)
℗ 2021 TwizzyRich
Notes:
(June 10, 2021) (4L is officially released)
(August 5, 2021) (Trëndi is officially released)
Type: Trëndi is Yeat’s third release of 2021, following June’s 4L and April’s Alivë. Released on August 5, 2021, it serves as a teaser for his project, Up 2 Më. Consisting of four tracks, the EP features Yeat’s TwizzyRich labelmate, SeptembersRich, and boasts production from BNYX®, Sharkboy, Rio Leyva, and more. The EP gained attention due to its two singles, “Mad bout that” and “Fukit.” Technically speaking, this era can be treated as a sub-era for Up 2 Më.

Name:
Up 2 Më
℗ 2021 TwizzyRich
Notes:
(August 5, 2021) (Trëndi is officially released)
(September 10, 2021) (Up 2 Më is officially released)
Type: Up 2 Më is Yeat’s third independently-released album of 2021 and his fourth release of the year, following April’s Alivë, June’s 4L, and August’s Trëndi. As he explained in an interview with Our Generation Music, the album’s title represents his control over his life and success, “It’s up to me to go up”. Up 2 Më features a multitude of hard-hitting electronic beats layered over Yeat rapping about his extensive drug use, newfound wealth, and his influx of women. Thanks to the viral success of the album’s lead single, “Gët Busy,” Yeat signed a deal with Interscope Records for one million dollars, resulting in this album being Yeat’s final independent release.

(September 10, 2021) (Up 2 Më is officially released)
(February 18, 2022) (2 Alivë is officially released)
Type: 2 Alivë is the sequel to Yeat’s April 2021 project, Alivë, and his first album released through Interscope Records. The album was first teased on December 13, 2021, where Yeat shared the project’s official cover art via social media, teasing fans with a January 2022 release date, however, the album missed the initial deadline. Then on January 31, 2022, Yeat announced in both an Instagram Story and an Instagram post that he had turned the project in. He teased that it would be released between February 11 and February 18. Yeat released the project’s lead single, “Still countin,” along with a music video directed by Cole Bennett on February 11, 2022.

(prod. BenjiCold)
Notes: Track 1 on 2 Alivë. Previewed by Yeat in an Instagram post captioned "#####" on October 17, 2021 and in on Instagram Live in 2021. The original version was nearly a minute and a half longer than the released version, including an extended verse, minor differences in the ad-libs and beat, and an alternate outro. Filename for finished version previewed by Yeat as track 4 while previewing "Luh gëek" in an early 2 Alivë tracklist in an Instagram Story. Cole Bennett, the director for Lyrical Lemonade, mentioned in an interview released on March 21, 2022 with Our Generation Music that the track was originally around 5 minutes long, and that recommended to Yeat that he shorten the song to accommodate to fans' short attention spans, which he eventually did and used for the music video. Leaked two days before the album released.

Type: 2 Alivë (Geëk Pack) is the deluxe version of Yeat’s album, 2 Alivë. On February 16, 2022, Yeat announced in an Instagram Story that a deluxe version of 2 Alivë was on the way. In an Instagram groupchat with SeptembersRich, Yeat shared that Lil Uzi Vert will be featured on the deluxe album. On March 30, 2022, Yeat posted the cover art and the tracklist to Instagram, revealing that the project would include features from Lancey Foux and Lil Uzi Vert. The entire album was leaked two days before its official release, on March 30, 2022.

(September 9, 2022) (Lyfë is officially released)
Type: Lyfë is Yeat’s fifth extended play, following his April 2022 deluxe album 2 Alivë (Geëk Pack). First teased by Yeat in an Instagram Story on June 4, 2022, captioned: "Lyfë?" On June 15, 2022, in a now-deleted Tweet, Yeat confirmed this EP would precede the eponymous album, “Fin drop like a 8 song EP den ALBUM [LYFË]…. N change earth n music.”.  In an array of different Instagram Stories posted on August 24, 2022, Yeat would uncover the EP’s twelve-track runtime, as well as the lead single “Talk.” On the night of “Talk”’s release, Yeat would ultimately confirm the EP’s September 9 release date through an Instagram post.

Type: AftërLyfe is the third studio album by American rapper Yeat. It was released on February 24, 2023, by Geffen Records, Field Trip Recordings, and Twizzy Rich. The follow-up to his EP Lyfë, it contains a sole guest appearance from YoungBoy Never Broke Again as well as Yeat's alter egos Kranky Kranky & Luh Geeky.

Name:
2093
℗ 2024 Field Trip Recordings/Capitol Records
Notes:
(February 24, 2023) (AftërLyfe is officially released)
(February 16, 2024) (2093 officially releases)
Type: 2093 is Yeat’s 4th studio album. On October 2, 2023 he announced on the label Instagram account that he had been sober for 15 days and that the album was coming soon. From this point on there was a drastic change in his direction, going for a more "futuristic" sound, with the title "2093" taking direct inspiration from the 2009 movie Mr. Nobody, which takes place in 2092. On January 31, 2024, he announced the album would drop in February. On February 14, he confirmed the album for February 16.

(idgaf, IDBGNF, whatever i want)
Notes: Track 7 on For All The Dogs by Drake. Drake shared to his Instagram Story a picture of himself faceswapped into Yeat, captioned "Dialed In," acknowledged by BNYX® and Cole Bennett. Song previewed on September 13, 2023 in a Nike NOCTA ad for "the NOCTA Glider," where Drake captioned "Music by Twizz and The Boy." Yeat acknowledged the post by sharing on the label page's story, captioned "4allthadawgz....tbh." Teased for release on Drake's album, FOR ALL THE DOGS, originally set to release September 22, 2023, pushed back to October 6, 2023. Announced as track 7 the day before release. Released with an intro produced by Harley Arsenault sampling "The Tunnel" by Azimuth. After being released with poor mixing and other issues, the track was updated streaming services on October 16, 2023. Four days later, they fixed other issues and finalized the track on October 20, 2023.

On March 14, Yeat changed his Instagram bio to "3093." In late March, he changed his Instagram bio to "#LS," then he posted a picture in an Instagram Story with the caption "Lyfestyle." On April 10, 2024, Yeat announced in an Instagram Story that he was going to drop 2 more albums this year to "show u who do what," LYFESTYLE and A DANGEROUS LYFE. On September 23, 2024, he confirmed the album would drop in October, before eventually confirming the October 18 date.

Name:
Childish Gambino - Cruisin' [V1]
(feat. Yeat) (prod. Dahi, Ludwig Göransson, Donald Glover, Michael Uzowuru & Caroline Whitaker)
(Cruisin' Thru Africa)
Notes: Track 12 on Bando Stone and The New World by Childish Gambino. Childish Gambino originally began working on the track in 2014. A rough idea version with mumble, some finished parts and an open verse allegedly some time between 2017-2018 was listed for sale by TheSource on May 5, 2021 and forceleaked years later on April 17, 2023, leaking later in higher bitrate on December 25, 2023. On January 7, 2024, Yeat posted an Instagram Story captioned "me n gambino going chips for grass and yall would never know," first teasing a Childish Gambino collaboration. On January 31, Yeat announced that his album 2093 was going to drop in February in an Instagram slideshow captioned "2093ISCOMING...... 2.??.42," including an image of him in the studio with Childish Gambino. On February 24, 2024, Yeat posted the credits of "Power Trip" with Donald Glover's name in an Instagram Story captioned "Dont worry we have more for yall," indicating at least another collaboration between the two. This track was performed by Childish Gambino at the Little Island Listening Party on July 6, 2024, featuring finished vocals compared to his previous versions, new instrumentation, and a guest feature from Yeat.

Name:
FATË (BONUS)
(prod. 730hahah, Darkoivx, Amorii, Synthetic & LRBG)
(fate, See a thousand, 1000, drv, drive)
Notes: Track 22 and bonus on LYFESTYLE. Instrumental uploaded to 730hahah's BeatStars page on May 25, 2023. Previewed by Yeat in an Instagram Carousel post captioned ............".###1000" on June 25, 2023. Co-producer 730hahah confirmed the song was scrapped from the then-upcoming album, 2093, in an Instagram Live on January 19, 2024. Filename "fate prod 730 " and runtime of 2:23 also previewed by 730hahah in an Instagram Live, however, unknown if legit. In a deleted Instagram post from April 17, 2024, a fan replied to Yeat "drop fate big bro," which Yeat liked and replied with "🤞," teasing a potential future release. On August 20, 2024, Instagram DMs from a groupchat between Yeat and fanpages leaked, in which Yeat confirmed that he is dropping the song on the second iteration of Lyfëstyle: "Fate gone make LS 🤫." Due to the popular fan name "Fate" for the highly-anticipated track, Yeat decided to rename it. The digital album version of the track was exported on August 31, 2024. On September 17, 2024, Yeat subtly teased that "FATE" would be releasing as (originally) the 22nd track on the album as a "bonus" while teasing "LYFE PARTY" as an outro: "Bonus "FATË (BONUS)."" The track was updated from the original 2023 version with an outro produced by Synthetic and LRBG.

(October 18, 2024) (LYFESTYLE officially releases)
(October 24, 2024) (LYFESTYLE DIGITAL DELUXE officially releases)
Type: The LYFESTYLE DIGITAL DELUXE was exclusively delivered as a digital album for purchase on October 24, 2024. On October 21, 2024, Yeat first teased a "surprise" in an Instagram Story in response to LYFESTYLE merchandise nearly selling out: "EVERYTHING IS ALMOST GON3 SURPRISE WHEN IT SELLS OUT?????!??." The next day, he announced a digital deluxe album in an Instagram Story: "GRA1LS ONLY AVAILABLE FOR 48 HOURS YALL WENT TOO CRAZY SURPR1SE SURPR1SE I LOVE YOU....❤️" and the three bonus tracks were revealed the next day.

Name:
DANGEROUS SUMMER
(A DANGEROUS LYFE [V1-V3])
Lyfestyle Corporation/Field Trip Recordings/Capitol Records;
℗ 2025 Field Trip Recordings and UMG Recordings, Inc.
Notes:
(October 24, 2024) (LYFESTYLE DIGITAL DELUXE officially releases)
(August 1, 2025) (DANGEROUS SUMMER is officially released)
Type: DANGEROUS SUMMER is an EP ahead of A DANGEROUS LYFE. Starting from May 24, 2025, Yeat seemed to begin to start somewhat of a rollout of the album, repeating the phrase "DANGEROUS SUMMER" again and again, teasing a release sometime in the summer of 2025. This period of time saw Yeat and his entourage traveling around the globe and performing at several concerts. On July 26, 2025, BNYX® confirmed in a now-deleted post on X (formerly Twitter): "A DANGEROUS SUMMER otw ⚠️ the tape is classic," clarifying "ADL is a full album." Announced by Yeat 2 days later.

Name:
IM YEAT
(with BNYX®) (prod. BNYX® & Sapjer)
(I'M YEAT)
Notes: Lead single for DANGEROUS SUMMER, track 7 on the EP. Recorded in a session in Saint-Tropez, France on July 3, 2025 with BNYX® and Sapjer, with the pair reusing a sample that BNYX® used in an instrumental he produced live on Twitch on February 14, 2025. Performed live by Yeat during his set during splash! Festival on July 5, 2025 in Ferropolis, Germany. BNYX® later confirmed on X (formerly Twitter) that "[they] made that 48 hours ago." Later the same day, Yeat posted a clip of his performance in an Instagram Story captioned "IMYEAT ... LETSGET DANGEROUS ⚠️," and teases again on X: "IM YEAT." Performed live again at BEACH, PLEASE! in Costinești, Romania on July 10, 2025. Previewed again in an Instagram Story on July 11, 2025. Single announced in an Instagram post on July 15, 2025, captioned: "IM YEAT….. FRIDAY….. ⚠️💿 @bnyx."

Name:
ADL
(𝕬 𝕯𝖆𝖓𝖌𝖊𝖗𝖔𝖚𝖘 𝕷𝖞𝖋𝖊 / 𝒜 𝒟𝒶𝓃𝑔𝑒𝓇𝑜𝓊𝓈 𝐿𝑜𝓋𝑒)
Lyfestyle Corporation/Field Trip Recordings/Capitol Records;
℗ 2026 Field Trip Recordings and UMG Recordings, Inc.
Notes:
(August 1, 2025) (DANGEROUS SUMMER is officially released)
(March 26, 2026) (ADL is officially released)
Type: ADL, which stands for A Dangerous Lyfe and A Dangerous Love, is Yeat's sixth studio album. It is his longest worked on project, having been conceptualized for nearly two years. Following the release of DANGEROUS SUMMER, Yeat's full focus is back to ADL. During this period, Yeat worked extensively, collaborating with new artists and working in different studios, including Rick Rubin's Shangri-La. In March 2026, Yeat finally announced the release date of the album and officially revealed the project was going to be a double album, not solely A Dangerous Lyfe, but also A Dangerous Love, which Yeat had left hints of throughout the process, dating back over a year in advance.

Name:
BNYX®, Bizarrap, Yeat & Peso Pluma - fuëgo
(prod. BNYX® & Bizarrap)
(ALL U WANT, Juice)
Notes: Fourth single for GENESIS FM by BNYX® and track 7 on the album. Confirmed by BNYX® in a TikTok Live on May 14, 2026 to have been started in June or July of 2025. Originally listed for sale on January 27, 2026 and was privatebought. Music video teased by BNYX® in an Instagram post on his birthday on April 23, 2026, confirming the track's placement on his upcoming album. BNYX® revealed the tracklist for the album via his socials on May 7, 2026, announcing the title of the track. Bizarrap commented "dime si te kema kema" on the Instagram post, confirming that the track was in fact the same as the leaked snippet. Previewed by BNYX® in an Instagram post on May 11, 2026, confirming that the track would be released as a single two days before album release.
